regions.name: Tokyo
regions.description: Japan's capital — a dazzling mix of ultramodern skyscrapers, ancient temples, and world-class food
regions.bestFor: First-time visitors, urban exploration, technology, nightlife

regions.name: Kyoto
regions.description: Japan's cultural heart with over 1,600 Buddhist temples, traditional geisha districts, and bamboo groves

regions.name: Osaka
regions.description: Japan's culinary capital and entertainment hub — famous for street food, comedy, and Osaka Castle

attractions.name: Senso-ji Temple
attractions.description: Tokyo's oldest and most visited Buddhist temple, dating to 628 AD, in the historic Asakusa district

attractions.name: Shibuya Crossing
attractions.description: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing — a defining Tokyo experience, especially at night

attractions.name: Meiji Shrine
attractions.description: Serene Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji, surrounded by 170 acres of forested parkland

attractions.name: teamLab Planets
attractions.description: Immersive digital art museum in Toyosu with stunning water and light installations

attractions.name: Shinjuku Gyoen
attractions.description: Stunning national garden spanning 144 acres — perfect for cherry blossoms in April

attractions.name: Ueno Park & Museums
attractions.description: Tokyo's premier cultural park with multiple world-class museums, zoo, and Ueno Tosho-gu shrine

attractions.name: Fushimi Inari Taisha
attractions.description: Iconic shrine with thousands of vermillion torii gates winding up Mount Inari — best at dawn

attractions.name: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
attractions.description: Otherworldly path through towering bamboo stalks in western Kyoto — stunning at golden hour

attractions.name: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
attractions.description: Zen temple whose top two floors are completely covered in gold leaf, reflecting in a pond

attractions.name: Gion District
attractions.description: Kyoto's famous geisha district with preserved machiya townhouses, teahouses, and evening geiko sightings

attractions.name: Osaka Castle
attractions.description: Magnificent 16th-century castle surrounded by a large park — panoramic views from the top floor

attractions.name: Dotonbori
attractions.description: Osaka's iconic entertainment strip along the canal — neon signs, street food, and the famous Glico Running Man

april.weather: Mild and pleasant, 10-20°C, cherry blossom season
pros: Iconic cherry blossoms in full bloom

july.weather: Hot and humid, 25-35°C, rainy season early July

october.weather: Comfortable and sunny, 15-25°C, early autumn colors
cons: Prices rise as autumn peak approaches

culturalTips: Remove shoes when entering homes, traditional ryokan, and many restaurants — look for the step up
Do not tip — it can be considered rude or confusing; exceptional service is expected as standard
Bow slightly when greeting, thanking, or apologizing — a 15-degree bow is fine for tourists
Carry cash — many smaller restaurants and rural shops do not accept cards
Buy a Suica or Pasmo IC card at any major train station for seamless travel on trains and buses
Keep voices low on trains and public transport; phone calls in train carriages are discouraged
Use two hands when receiving a business card or a gift — a sign of respect
Eating and drinking while walking is generally frowned upon outside festival stalls

budget.description: Excellent transport links, abundant capsule hotels and guesthouses at $50-80/night

mid.description: Central location, 3-star business hotels, easy access to all major sites, $120-200/night

luxury.description: Premium hotels with concierge, spa, and stunning city or garden views, $300-600/night
